customer_phrases: Wet vs dry sanding
You need to choose whether to sand wet or dry for the current stage.
Use dry sanding for shaping and early defect removal. Use wet sanding for finer refinement from 600 grit and above when the surface can be rinsed safely.

Confusion comes from mixing removal and finishing stages without a clear method plan.
Dry stage: 120 to 320. Wet stage: 600 to 1000 and finer.
Do not start wet sanding too early or use dirty slurry between grit steps.
Do not use wet sanding for heavy stock removal.
Do not switch methods mid-step before the scratch pattern is even.
Do not use contaminated water in fine stages.

title: How to move through grits correctly
description: Grit progression is unclear and scratches keep carrying into finer steps.
customer_phrases: How to move through grits correctly
Grit progression is unclear and scratches keep carrying into finer steps.
Use small grit jumps and fully remove each scratch pattern before moving finer. A common path is 120, 180, 220, 320, then 400 or finer as needed.

Scratch transfer happens when grit jumps are too large or step completion is inconsistent.
120 to 180 to 220 to 320 to 400; continue finer only when needed.
Do not skip from coarse grit directly to fine finishing grit.
Do not move on before the current step is fully even.
Do not use large grit jumps on visible-finish surfaces.
Do not ignore lighting checks between stages.
